description: To regard with deep respect and honor; veneration that recognizes worth. 'Relu' names the honoring gaze—seeing and acknowledging what is worthy of respect.
sound_symbolism: The word honors from the ground up. 'Ne' begins with grounded clarity, respect rooted in honest recognition. 'Lu' flows with deep feeling. Reverence as deep, grounded honor rather than mere performance.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi nelu' (I revere). Takes object of reverence. Related to 'thunoa' (dignified) and 'rolia' (worth).

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reverence for elders—traditional honoring of those who carry wisdom.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Reverence without attachment—honoring without grasping or idolizing.
